title: Pipe-first rewrite of a deeply nested transformation
prompt:
Rewrite this 30-line imperative function as a pipe pipeline:

```
def analyze(events) do
  filtered = Enum.filter(events, fn e -> e.type == :click end)
  grouped = Enum.group_by(filtered, fn e -> e.user_id end)
  counts = Enum.map(grouped, fn {uid, es} -> {uid, length(es)} end)
  sorted = Enum.sort_by(counts, fn {_, n} -> n end, :desc)
  top = Enum.take(sorted, 10)
  result = Enum.map(top, fn {uid, n} -> %{user_id: uid, clicks: n} end)
  result
end
```

Output a pipe pipeline from `events` through all transformations. No intermediate variables. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Analytics do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Six-step pipeline. Must also fold the final `Enum.map` transformation correctly. Sonnet often leaves at least one intermediate.
